article_meta.title: By Ellie CookSenior Defense ReporterShareNewsweek is a Trust Project memberU.S

reasoning: The model correctly identifies three genuine bias instances all in the author's own prose. No quoted text is flagged as author bias. 'Rambling speech' is indeed a subjective qualifying adjective from the author. 'Deepening the yawning chasm' is dramatic/sensationalist author prose. 'Has always been unfathomable, until now' is an editorial opinion presented as fact by the author. The bias type labels are all correctly applied. The model appropriately excludes Trump's quoted statements and attributed statements from alliance officials.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All three flagged instances are clearly from the author's own prose. No quoted text or attributed statements were flagged. Perfect compliance with the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: All three flagged items are defensible genuine bias patterns in the author's voice. 'Rambling' is clearly subjective, 'yawning chasm' is sensationalist metaphor, and 'unfathomable, until now' is editorial framing.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model caught the main instances. One could argue 'The White House had refused to rule out using military force to take Greenland, which would be a violation of international law' contains author editorializing (presenting 'violation of international law' as settled fact without attribution), but this is arguably factual and defensible. Minor miss at most.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All three labels are correctly applied: 'rambling' as Subjective Qualifying Adjectives, 'yawning chasm' as Sensationalism/Emotionalism, and 'unfathomable, until now' as Opinion Statements Presented as Fact. These are all accurate categorizations.
